steps.title: Find the greatest common factor (GCF)
steps.content: Look at the three terms in $7x^{5}-6x^{4}+9x^{3}$. The coefficients 7, -6, and 9 share no common factor. The powers of $x$ are 5, 4, and 3 — the highest power common to all terms is $x^3$. So the GCF is $x^3$.

steps.title: Factor out the GCF and match to the choices
steps.content:
Divide each term by $x^3$:
  • $7x^{5} \div x^3 = 7x^2$
  • $-6x^{4} \div x^3 = -6x$
  • $9x^{3} \div x^3 = 9$
The factored expression is $x^{3}(7x^{2}-6x+9)$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
